title: Counting Bits
description:
## Counting Bits

Given an integer `n`, return an array `ans` of length `n + 1` such that for each `i` (0 <= i <= n), `ans[i]` is the number of `1` bits in the binary representation of `i`.

**Constraints:**
- `0 <= n <= 10^5`

solutions.label: Naive Popcount — O(n log n)
solutions.explanation: For each number from 0 to n, count its set bits by checking each bit. Each popcount call takes O(log i) time.

solutions.label: Dynamic Programming — O(n)
solutions.explanation: Key insight: `i >> 1` is just `i` with the last bit removed (equivalent to `i // 2`). So `dp[i] = dp[i >> 1] + (i & 1)`: the bit count of i equals the bit count of i//2 plus 1 if i is odd. This recurrence fills the table in O(n) with no division or repeated bit-checking.

solutions.label: Dynamic Programming — O(n)
solutions.explanation: dp[i] = dp[i >> 1] + (i & 1): bit count of i equals bit count of i//2 plus its last bit.

hints: For each i, count its bits by checking i & 1 and shifting right — but this is O(n log n) total.
O(n) trick: dp[i] = dp[i >> 1] + (i & 1). Shifting right removes the last bit; the recurrence builds on already-computed results.
